title: Evolve schemas additively and versioned
description: Add nullable columns and new fields rather than renaming or retyping, so existing consumers keep working through the change.
body: Use when a source or table schema must change. Do not rename, drop, or retype a column in place — every downstream reader that expects the old shape breaks at once. Actions: add new columns as nullable with defaults, dual-write during a transition, migrate consumers, then retire the old field; coordinate with the data contract. Validation signals include downstream jobs parsing both old and new records and a schema registry accepting the change as backward-compatible. Risk boundary: a breaking schema change cascades across every consumer; for shared tables, require consumer sign-off and a rollback path before deploying.
